response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and web developer with 15+ years of software engineering history, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The position requires native-level Japanese, which the candidate does not possess at all — a hard disqualifier. Beyond the language barrier, the role demands deep expertise in embedded C programming, Linux kernel internals, and networking security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, Layer-2/3 switching) that are entirely absent from his resume. His background is concentrated in TypeScript, Node.js, and web/cloud platforms, with only peripheral exposure to C and embedded systems early in his career.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, embedded, and systems contexts demonstrates general engineering maturity
Fluent English enables reading of technical documentation and email communication as required
Some early exposure to C/C++ at National Instruments and in personal projects indicates a non-zero baseline with lower-level languages
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — this is listed as a hard non-negotiable requirement for the role
Zero demonstrated experience in networking security domains: no MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, Layer-2/3 switching, or related embedded security work
C programming is marginal and dated; primary skills are TypeScript, Node.js, and web technologies with no evidence of Linux kernel analysis or modification
